goal: Build the meeting-view MVP in client/: a page-gated, Zoom-style meeting that fetches a briefing from GET /api/briefings/:id and presents one slide at a time on a slide stage, with info/question/decision turn-taking and a progress indicator.

slides.title: Lane 1 — Hash router + landing links
content: Spec: client-router — all 6 acceptance criteria met, reviewer PASS
client/src/Router.jsx: #/ -> BriefingList, #/meeting/:id -> MeetingView
Defensive parse: non-integer id redirects to #/; unknown hash falls through to list
App.jsx wraps each briefing row in <a href='#/meeting/{id}'>; Back link above the meeting view
Delta vs spec: zero — no router package added; routing logic lives in Router.jsx imported by main.jsx
Delta vs first attempt: build had hard-failed on a missing MeetingView import; fixed by adding the shell + wiring vitest
slides.narration: First lane: the client-side router. We went with hash routing, so no router dependency and no server changes. The route table is simple — hash slash goes to the briefing list, and hash slash meeting slash id goes to the meeting view, with the id parsed as an integer. The parsing is deliberately defensive: a non-integer id redirects back to the list, and any unrecognised hash just falls through to the list rather than crashing. On the landing page, every briefing row is now an anchor linking to its meeting at hash slash meeting slash id, and there's a Back link above the meeting view. The implementation matches the spec exactly — six of six criteria, reviewer pass, and no router package added. Worth noting one stumble: the first build attempt hard-failed because Router imported a MeetingView file that didn't exist yet, so we added a minimal shell and wired up vitest; integration later swapped that shell for the real wired page.

slides.title: Lane 2 — Meeting view page
content: Spec: meeting-view-page — all 6 acceptance criteria met, reviewer PASS (10 tests)
client/src/MeetingView.jsx: fetches GET /api/briefings/:id on mount, re-fetches on id change
States: loading (data-testid=loading), error (data-testid=error, text contains 'not found'/'error'), ok
Renders sprint header (sprintId, goal) above the stage; drives useMeetingState(briefing.slides)
Wires SlideStage with all 6 props: slides, currentIndex, answers, onContinue, onAnswer, onDecide
Delta vs spec: zero — no POST, no write endpoint; a cancelled-flag guards stale fetches
Scope note: malformed payload (no slides array) is treated as an error state
slides.narration: Second lane: the meeting view page itself. MeetingView takes an id prop, fetches the briefing from get slash api slash briefings slash id on mount, and re-fetches if the id changes. It has three states — loading, error, and ok — each with the test ids the spec asked for, and the error copy contains both 'not found' and 'error' so either matcher hits. Once the briefing loads, it renders the sprint header — the sprint id and the goal — above the stage, then hands the slides to the page-gating hook and renders the slide stage with all six props wired exactly as specified. There are no writes anywhere; a cancelled flag guards against a stale fetch landing after the id changed. The only judgment call: a malformed payload missing a slides array is treated as an error rather than rendering an empty stage. Ten tests, all green, reviewer pass.

slides.title: Lane 3 — Slide stage renderer
content: Spec: slide-stage-renderer — all 8 acceptance criteria met, reviewer PASS (12 tests)
client/src/SlideStage.jsx: purely presentational, default export, no fetch, no data state
Renders title / content <li> bullets / narration with data-testid slide-title|slide-content|slide-narration
Progress indicator 'Slide N of M' (data-testid=slide-progress)
info -> Continue; question -> textarea+Submit (disabled until non-empty, sends trimmed text); decision -> Approve + Redirect (Redirect disabled until direction typed)
Boundaries: empty/out-of-range slide -> 'No slide' placeholder; unknown type -> content only + console.warn
Delta vs spec: zero
slides.narration: Third lane: the slide stage renderer. This is the purely presentational piece — no fetching, no data state, all turn-taking flows through callbacks it receives as props. It renders the slide title, the content bullets as list items, and the presenter narration, each tagged with the test ids the spec named, plus a 'Slide N of M' progress indicator. The controls are driven by the slide type: an info slide shows a Continue button; a question slide shows a textarea and a Submit button that stays disabled until you type something and sends the trimmed text; and the decision slide shows Approve plus a Redirect that's disabled until you've typed a direction. Edge cases are handled cleanly — an empty or out-of-range slide shows a 'No slide' placeholder, and an unrecognised slide type renders just the content and logs a warning rather than throwing. Eight criteria, twelve tests, zero delta.

slides.title: Lane 4 — Page-gating state machine
content: Spec: meeting-state-machine — all 9 acceptance criteria met, reviewer PASS (17 tests)
client/src/useMeetingState.js: returns { currentIndex, answers, decision, continue, answer, decide }
continue() advances only on info slides; no-op elsewhere
answer(text) hard-gates question slides: records answers[index] then advances; answer('')/whitespace is a no-op
decide(outcome, direction) sets decision and does NOT advance (decision slide is terminal)
Index clamped at slides.length-1; empty/undefined slides are safe no-ops; only useState/useCallback used
Delta vs spec: zero — no fetch, no storage, all state client-side
slides.narration: Fourth lane: the page-gating state machine — the rules that decide when the meeting can move forward. It's a React hook returning the current index, the captured answers, the decision, and three actions. Continue only advances on info slides and is a no-op anywhere else. Answer hard-gates the question slides — it records your answer by slide index and advances, but an empty or whitespace-only answer does nothing, so you genuinely can't skip past a question. Decide records the outcome on the terminal decision slide and deliberately does not advance, because that slide closes the meeting. The index is clamped at the last slide, empty or undefined slide arrays are safe no-ops, and the whole thing uses only useState and useCallback — no fetch, no storage, everything stays in client state. Nine criteria, seventeen tests, zero delta from the spec.

slides.title: Lane 5 — Client Vitest setup + suite
content: Spec: client-test-setup — all 7 acceptance criteria met, reviewer PASS
client/package.json: 'test': 'vitest run'; devDeps vitest, @testing-library/react+user-event+jest-dom, jsdom
client/vitest.config.js: jsdom environment, globals:true, react plugin, setup.js registers jest-dom matchers
Tests cover SlideStage renderer + useMeetingState gating, driven by the real briefings/sprint-1.json fixture
Lane shipped placeholder SlideStage/useMeetingState (named exports) to verify in isolation
Delta vs spec: those placeholders collided with canonical lanes; integrator dropped them, kept the unique useMeetingState.test.js
Server test untouched: cd server && npm test -> 1 pass, 0 fail
slides.narration: Fifth lane: the client test harness. This added the test script and dev dependencies to the client package, a vitest config running in jsdom with globals on, and a setup file that registers the jest-dom matchers. The suites cover both the renderer and the gating state machine, driven by the real sprint-one briefing as a fixture. One honest wrinkle here: to make its tests runnable in isolation, this lane shipped its own placeholder copies of SlideStage and useMeetingState as named exports — which would have collided with, and were incompatible with, the canonical components from lanes three and four. The integrator resolved that by dropping the placeholders in favour of the real implementations and keeping this lane's unique state-machine test. The existing server test was never touched and still passes, one pass, zero fail.

slides.title: Integration & test results
content: All 5 branches merged to main on the first checks pass — no fix-loop iterations needed
Canonical-owner strategy resolved heavy overlap (every lane scaffolded package.json/vitest config/docs)
client/MeetingView.jsx: kept the wired version over client-router's shell stub
client/SlideStage.jsx + useMeetingState.js: kept canonical lanes over client-test-setup placeholders
Client: 5 test files, 55 tests passing (incl. a duplicate useMeetingState suite at two paths)
Server: node --test -> 1 test, 1 pass, 0 fail. npm run build: vite OK in ~190ms
Commit range e651bd2..2c61d36
slides.narration: Pulling it together: all five branches merged into main on the first checks pass — no fix-loop iterations were needed, build and tests were green on attempt one. The lanes overlapped heavily because each one independently scaffolded the package file, the vitest config, and the docs, and several reimplemented the same components to test in isolation. The integrator handled that by picking one canonical owner per file: the wired MeetingView over the router's shell stub, and the real SlideStage and state hook over the test lane's placeholders. The result is fifty-five client tests passing across five files — note there's a harmless duplicate state-machine suite living at two paths, which I'd flag as a cleanup. The server test still passes, one of one, and the build is green in about a hundred and ninety milliseconds. End to end: meeting slash id is reachable, the landing list links each briefing, and one slide shows at a time with info-continue, required answers, and an approve-or-redirect decision — answers and decision stay in client state only, honoring every scope limit.

slides.title: Docs changed
content: docs/README.md current-state section updated by the in-lane docs agents
Heading 'Current state (kickoff)' -> 'Current state' (stale label removed)
client/ entry expanded: hash router, App.jsx links, MeetingView fetch+states
Added entries for SlideStage (props, data-testids, per-type controls) and useMeetingState (signature, gate rules, client-state-only)
Added styles.css class list and client-tests entry (vitest/jsdom, fixture path, run command)
'Not yet built' rewritten: /api/minutes, /api/next-sprint, /api/qa, voice/TTS/ASR, next-sprint wiring
Single doc file, no contradictions, no history notes (docs-maintainer convention)
slides.narration: On docs: the in-lane docs agents kept docs slash README current as the work landed, and the integrator did a final reconciliation pass. The stale 'kickoff' label in the heading is gone. The client section now describes the hash router, the landing-list links, and the meeting view's fetch and loading-error-ok states, with dedicated entries for the slide stage — its props, test ids, and per-type controls — and the state hook — its signature, gate rules, and client-state-only constraint. There's a styles entry listing the new CSS classes and a client-tests entry naming the vitest-jsdom setup, the fixture path, and the run command. The 'not yet built' list was rewritten to name only what's genuinely still ahead: minutes, next-sprint, the Q-and-A endpoint, voice, and the next-sprint wiring. It's a single doc file with no contradictions and no history notes, per the docs-maintainer convention.

slides.title: Decision: approve and pick next sprint
content: Sprint-2 complete: meeting-view MVP shipped, tested (55 client tests), documented, merged to main
Reviewer verdicts: 5/5 lanes PASS; no escalations, no unresolved items, no open questions
Proposed next (Sprint 3): close the human-direction loop — POST /api/minutes on decision resolve + GET /api/next-sprint + scripts/poll.mjs
Cleanup candidate to fold in: dedupe the two useMeetingState test files (src/ vs __tests__/) into one canonical suite
Approve to proceed with the minutes/next-sprint loop, or Redirect to a different next work item
slides.narration: That wraps Sprint two. The meeting-view MVP is shipped, tested with fifty-five client tests, documented, and merged to main — all five lanes passed review, with no escalations, no unresolved items, and no open questions blocking you. For the next sprint I'd propose closing the human-direction loop from the HTTP contract: writing minutes via post slash api slash minutes when you resolve a decision slide, adding get slash api slash next-sprint, and implementing the local poller so your direction in the meeting becomes the next sprint's input. I'd also fold in one small cleanup — there are two copies of the state-machine test at different paths, and we should collapse them into one canonical suite. If that plan sounds right, approve to proceed; otherwise redirect me and tell me precisely what you'd rather tackle next, and I'll re-plan around it.

artifacts.specDeltas: client-router (PASS, specWrong:false): delta NONE — 6/6 AC. #/meeting/42 renders MeetingView (Router.jsx parses integer id); #/ and empty hash render BriefingList; each row is <a href='#/meeting/{id}'> (App.jsx); Back link href='#/'; no router package; Router.jsx imported by main.jsx. First build attempt hard-failed (Router imported missing MeetingView.jsx) — fixed by adding shell + vitest config. meeting-view-page (PASS): delta NONE — 6/6 AC, 10 tests. Fetches GET /api/briefings/:id on mount (id in useEffect deps); loading/error/ok with data-testid; error text 'Briefing not found, or an error occurred loading it.' contains both 'not found' and 'error'; drives useMeetingState(briefing.slides); wires SlideStage with all 6 props; no POST. slide-stage-renderer (PASS): delta NONE — 8/8 AC, 12 tests. Presentational; title/content<li>/narration data-testids; 'Slide N of M' progress; info=Continue, question=textarea+Submit(disabled empty, trimmed), decision=Approve onDecide('approve','')+Redirect(disabled empty); empty/out-of-range='No slide'; unknown type=content-only+console.warn. meeting-state-machine (PASS): delta NONE — 9/9 AC, 17 tests. Returns {currentIndex, answers, decision, continue, answer, decide}; continue() advances info only; answer(text) gates question, records by index, no-op on ''/whitespace; decide(outcome,direction) sets decision without advancing; index clamped at length-1; only useState/useCallback. client-test-setup (PASS): delta NONE for canonical deliverables — 7/7 AC. Added 'test':'vitest run', devDeps (vitest@^2.1.8, @testing-library/react@^16.1.0, user-event@^14.5.2, jsdom@^25.0.1), vitest.config.js (jsdom, globals:true). Shipped placeholder SlideStage/useMeetingState (named exports) for isolation; integrator dropped them for the canonical lanes, kept unique useMeetingState.test.js.
